subject: Geography
title: Stalactites and Stalagmites
description: A fast breakdown of Stalactites and Stalagmites for builders.

hook.text: Stalactites hang from ceilings. Stalagmites rise from floors. They take thousands of years to grow.

buildup.text: Mineral-rich water drips through rock. Each drop leaves a tiny ring of calcite behind.

discovery.text: Over centuries, those rings build into stone icicles and towers, sometimes meeting in the middle.

twist.text: Scientists read stalactites like tree rings—each layer records the climate of its era.

climax.text: A stalactite is a geological diary, recording rainfall and temperature for millennia.

punchline.text: Every drip writes a line of Earth's climate history.

quiz.question: What information can scientists extract from stalactites?
options: Historical climate data like rainfall and temperature
